snapshot.scope: Independent Product Planning review binds the built-in desktop Agent's first-party filesystem, network, command and package capabilities to one submitted-task authority and one exact audit without duplicate prompts. The general command tool uses exact executable/argv execution, bounded output/time/cancellation and a reduced environment; ordinary connected-provider task context proceeds without a second confirmation. Restricted content, unknown or changed provider boundaries, irreversible destructive effects, credential disclosure and third-party self-authorization remain separate hard boundaries.

reviewers.task: Built-in OS capability and submitted-task authority review
reviewers.independence: Product Planning owns only documentation and independently compares the Development-owned command executor, capability adapter, Permission Broker and model-egress policy with task authority, secret handling, hard safety boundaries, recovery, IPC and test contracts.

dimensions.summary: Built-in OS capabilities stay main-owned and machine-local. Exact executable identity, argv, working directory, timeout and one-use task authority are bound without exposing raw command authority to the renderer.

PRD, runtime policy, API, Pi integration, privacy and security owners agree that submitting a task authorizes ordinary first-party OS effects and connected-provider context once, while hard boundaries remain explicit.

The change binds capability presence and duplicate-prompt removal without promoting unrelated Requirements, Exits or phases; full interactive terminal sessions and third-party grant defaults remain separate follow-ups.

Focused tests cover exact command execution, output and time bounds, cancellation, environment reduction, real Feishu CLI installation, Home tool use, task authority, third-party spoof rejection and context-egress policy.

Existing Pi, permission, runtime-policy, API, privacy and security owners absorb the decision; two bounded main services and the responsibility inventory avoid a renderer or parallel permission stack.

verification.command: Independent review of built-in OS capability and submitted-task authority
verification.evidence: Review confirms the Agent can use first-party filesystem, network, command and package capabilities without duplicate prompts while third-party self-authorization, restricted content and irreversible effects remain blocked or separately confirmed.

verification.command: npm run verify:docs && npm run verify:decisions && npm run verify:traceability
verification.evidence: Links, document map, decisions and locked claims pass with no Requirement, Exit or Phase promotion.

verification.command: npm run verify:contracts && npm run verify:independent-documentation-review && npm run verify:documentation-leanness
verification.evidence: CON-001 through CON-004, service inventory and focused command/permission/egress tests bind the implementation while the union recipe retains published product roots.

coordination.taskTitle: Built-in OS capability and submitted-task authority
coordination.compatibility: The candidate adds one bounded first-party command executor and removes duplicate first-party task/context prompts; it does not expose secrets, grant third-party packages authority, bypass restricted content, or change destructive, backup, restore or vault confirmations.
coordination.acknowledgement: Product Planning records the user-authoritative OS Agent model, keeps Development code/test ownership separate, and leaves interactive terminal sessions and broader third-party grant policy as explicit future work.

nonBlockingRisks: The bounded command runner has no persistent PTY or interactive stdin session; commands that require an interactive terminal may still need a dedicated follow-up capability.
Third-party packages and Skills remain unable to inherit Pige first-party task authority by naming convention and still require their own reviewed authority path.
